hero.title: Model Context Protocol
hero.subtitle: The universal connector between AI models and the world
hero.description: MCP is an open protocol that standardizes how applications provide context to LLMs. Think of it as a USB-C port for AI — one universal interface that works with any model and any tool.

intro.title: What is MCP?
intro.bodyText:
Before MCP every AI integration was a custom one-off: different APIs\, different auth schemes\, different data formats. Developers had to rebuild the same connectors over and over again.

MCP solves this with a single standard protocol for bidirectional communication between AI models (clients) and the systems they interact with (servers). Built on JSON-RPC 2.0\, it works over stdio\, HTTP/SSE\, or WebSockets.

card-tools.title: Tools
card-tools.description: Functions that an AI model can invoke to perform actions or retrieve data. The model decides when to call a tool based on its description. Examples: execute_sql()\, search_web()\, send_email(). Tools are the hands of an AI agent.

card-resources.title: Resources
card-resources.description: Read-only data sources exposed by the MCP server to the client. Resources let the AI read files\, database records\, or API responses without calling a function. The application decides which resources to expose and when.

card-prompts.title: Prompts
card-prompts.description: Pre-built reusable prompt templates that include context from resources and accept user-defined arguments. Prompts are surfaced in the UI as slash-commands. They standardize common patterns like code review\, summarization\, or translation.

principles.title: Core MCP Principles
principles.items: [Universal interface: one protocol works with Claude\, GPT\, Gemini and local models,Separation of concerns: the AI model and the data layer evolve independently,Security by design: servers expose only what they explicitly declare,Stateful sessions: context is maintained across multiple tool calls,Transport-agnostic: works over stdio\, HTTP+SSE\, or WebSockets,Extensible: new primitives can be added without breaking existing clients]

jsonrpc.label: JSON-RPC 2.0 · tools/call
